_meta.description: Complete parameter correlation matrix — all known relationships between OBD-II parameters based on automotive engineering principles, engine control system logic, physics of ICE, and manufacturer calibration strategies

field_notes.diagnosticWeight: 1-10, how important this correlation is for diagnosis (10 = highest)
field_notes.correlationType: positive | negative | proportional | inverse | conditional | complex

correlations.name: Load vs Throttle Position
correlations.description: Engine load should increase with throttle opening. This is the fundamental driver demand correlation.
correlations.formula: Load ≈ f(TPS, RPM, VE)
correlations.whenHealthy: Load increases smoothly with throttle opening
correlations.whenAbnormal: Throttle opens but load stays low, or load high despite low throttle
possibleCauses: Vacuum leak causing high load at closed throttle
Clogged air filter limiting load increase

correlations.name: MAF vs Calculated Load
correlations.description: MAF reading should match calculated engine load. Validates MAF sensor accuracy.
correlations.formula: MAF(g/s) ≈ (RPM × Displacement × VE × Air Density) / 3456
correlations.whenHealthy: MAF reading matches expected airflow for current load
correlations.whenAbnormal: MAF reading deviates significantly from expected value
possibleCauses: Air leak between MAF and throttle body
MAF sensor element contaminated with oil

correlations.name: RPM vs Vehicle Speed
correlations.description: RPM/Speed ratio indicates gear selection and transmission function.
correlations.formula: Speed = (RPM × Tire Circumference) / (Final Drive × Gear Ratio × 60)
correlations.whenHealthy: Consistent RPM/Speed ratio within each gear
correlations.whenAbnormal: RPM high for speed (slipping) or speed erratic (VSS issue)
possibleCauses: Incorrect tire size affecting speed reading

correlations.name: Combined Fuel Trim Health
correlations.description: STFT + LTFT indicates total fuel system correction. Both together reveal severity of fuel delivery deviations.
correlations.formula: Total Correction = STFT + LTFT (should be near 0%)
correlations.whenHealthy: Combined trims stay within ±10%
correlations.whenAbnormal: Combined trims exceed ±15%, indicating fuel system issue
possibleCauses: Positive trims: Vacuum leak, low fuel pressure, clogged injector, air leak after MAF
Negative trims: Leaking injector, high fuel pressure, saturated EVAP canister
Oscillating: O2 sensor response issue, injector balance problem

correlations.name: Bank 1 vs Bank 2 Fuel Trim
correlations.description: Bank trim comparison identifies bank-specific issues on V-engines.
correlations.formula: |LTFT_B1 - LTFT_B2| should be < 5%
correlations.whenHealthy: Both banks require similar fuel correction
correlations.whenAbnormal: One bank running significantly leaner/richer than other
possibleCauses: Intake manifold gasket leak on one bank
Exhaust leak on one bank before O2 sensor
Head gasket seepage on one bank
O2 sensor reading difference between banks

correlations.name: O2 Sensor vs Fuel Trim Response
correlations.description: O2 voltage changes should cause opposite fuel trim changes.
correlations.formula: High O2 (>0.7V) → Negative STFT, Low O2 (<0.3V) → Positive STFT
correlations.whenHealthy: Fuel trims respond correctly to O2 sensor readings
correlations.whenAbnormal: No fuel trim response to O2 changes, or wrong direction

correlations.name: Pre-Cat vs Post-Cat O2 Activity
correlations.description: Post-cat O2 should be much steadier than pre-cat O2. Rapid switching post-cat = degraded catalyst.
correlations.formula: Post-cat O2 switching rate should be < 10% of pre-cat rate
correlations.whenHealthy: Pre-cat O2 switches rapidly, post-cat O2 stays near 0.6V
correlations.whenAbnormal: Post-cat O2 mirrors pre-cat O2 activity

correlations.name: Timing Advance vs Engine Load
correlations.description: Timing should retard as load increases to prevent knock.
correlations.formula: Timing ≈ Base Timing + f(RPM) - f(Load) - Knock Retard
correlations.whenHealthy: Timing decreases smoothly as load increases
correlations.whenAbnormal: Excessive timing at high load (knock risk) or no timing at low load (power loss)
possibleCauses: MAF sensor causing incorrect load calculation

correlations.name: Timing Advance vs RPM
correlations.description: Timing should advance with RPM to optimize burn rate.
correlations.formula: Timing advance increases roughly 1° per 500 RPM
correlations.whenHealthy: Timing advances smoothly with RPM increase
correlations.whenAbnormal: Flat timing curve or erratic timing changes

correlations.name: Misfire Activity vs Fuel Trim
correlations.description: Misfires often correlate with fuel trim extremes. Lean misfire = high positive trim. Rich misfire = high negative trim.
correlations.formula: Lean misfire: High positive trim + misfire. Rich misfire: High negative trim + misfire
correlations.whenHealthy: No misfires with normal fuel trims
correlations.whenAbnormal: Misfires increase with fuel trim extremes
possibleCauses: Lean misfire: Vacuum leak, clogged injector, low fuel pressure
Rich misfire: Fouled plug, ignition weak, flooding
Random misfire: Ignition component failure, compression issue

correlations.name: Coolant vs Intake Air Temp Warm-up
correlations.description: Both temperatures should rise together during warm-up. ECT rises faster due to engine heat generation.
correlations.formula: ECT should rise faster than IAT, both starting near ambient
correlations.whenHealthy: ECT rises steadily, IAT follows with heat soak
correlations.whenAbnormal: ECT not rising (thermostat stuck open) or IAT stuck
possibleCauses: Head gasket leak affecting temp readings

correlations.name: Coolant vs Oil Temperature
correlations.description: Oil temp should track slightly behind coolant temp, running 10-20°C higher under load.
correlations.formula: Oil temp ≈ Coolant temp + 10-20°C under load
correlations.whenHealthy: Oil temp follows coolant, slightly higher under load
correlations.whenAbnormal: Oil temp significantly higher than coolant

correlations.name: Coolant Temp vs Fuel Trim During Warmup
correlations.description: Fuel trims should normalize as engine warms up. Cold = richer mixture. Warm = normalized trims.
correlations.formula: Cold: Richer mixture (negative trim adjustment). Hot: Normalized trims
correlations.whenHealthy: Trims stabilize as engine reaches operating temp
correlations.whenAbnormal: Trims don't stabilize or are extreme when warm

correlations.name: Catalyst Temp vs Engine Load
correlations.description: Catalyst temperature increases with sustained load. Above 900°C indicates misfire or rich condition.
correlations.formula: Cat temp rises 50-100°C above exhaust temp under load
correlations.whenHealthy: Cat temp rises with load but stays below 850°C
correlations.whenAbnormal: Cat temp excessive (>900°C) indicating misfire or rich condition

correlations.name: MAP vs Throttle Position
correlations.description: Manifold pressure should increase (toward atmospheric) with throttle opening.
correlations.formula: MAP approaches 101 kPa (atmospheric) as throttle opens
correlations.whenHealthy: MAP increases smoothly from ~30-40 kPa at idle toward 101 kPa at WOT
correlations.whenAbnormal: MAP stays high at closed throttle or doesn't reach atmospheric at WOT

correlations.name: MAF vs RPM at Steady State
correlations.description: MAF should increase roughly proportionally with RPM at constant load.
correlations.formula: MAF doubles when RPM doubles at same load
correlations.whenHealthy: Linear relationship between MAF and RPM
correlations.whenAbnormal: MAF doesn't scale with RPM properly

correlations.name: Boost Pressure vs Throttle (Turbo)
correlations.description: Boost should build with throttle opening on turbo vehicles. No boost = turbo/wastegate failure.
correlations.formula: Boost builds after ~2500 RPM, reaches target at WOT
correlations.whenHealthy: Smooth boost build matching throttle input
correlations.whenAbnormal: No boost, slow boost build, or overboost

correlations.name: Turbo RPM vs Engine RPM
correlations.description: Turbo shaft speed correlates with engine RPM and load.
correlations.formula: Turbo RPM = f(Engine RPM, Load, Wastegate Position)
correlations.whenHealthy: Turbo spools up proportionally with engine load
correlations.whenAbnormal: Turbo not spooling or overspeeding

correlations.name: EGR Command vs MAP Response
correlations.description: Opening EGR should increase MAP (reduce vacuum). No MAP change = EGR stuck closed.
correlations.formula: MAP increases 5-15 kPa when EGR opens 20-40%
correlations.whenHealthy: MAP rises when EGR commanded, returns when closed
correlations.whenAbnormal: No MAP change with EGR command

correlations.name: EGR Activity at Idle
correlations.description: EGR should be closed at idle for stability. Active EGR at idle causes rough running.
correlations.formula: EGR should be 0% at idle
correlations.whenHealthy: EGR stays closed at idle, RPM stable
correlations.whenAbnormal: EGR open at idle causing rough running

correlations.name: EVAP Purge vs Fuel Trim Response
correlations.description: Fuel trims should shift slightly rich when purging hydrocarbons from canister.
correlations.formula: STFT goes slightly negative (1-5%) when purge active
correlations.whenHealthy: Small negative fuel trim shift during purge
correlations.whenAbnormal: No fuel trim change or large rich shift during purge
possibleCauses: No change: Purge valve stuck closed, empty canister
Large shift: Saturated canister, liquid fuel in EVAP

correlations.name: Trans Fluid Temp vs Engine Load
correlations.description: Transmission temperature increases with sustained load.
correlations.formula: Trans temp rises 1-2°C per minute under sustained high load
correlations.whenHealthy: Trans temp stabilizes around 80-100°C normal driving
correlations.whenAbnormal: Trans temp climbing rapidly or exceeding 120°C

correlations.name: Gear Selection vs RPM/Speed Ratio
correlations.description: Each gear has a specific RPM/Speed ratio. Deviation indicates slipping or wrong gear.
correlations.formula: Expected RPM = Speed × Gear Ratio × Final Drive / Tire Rev
correlations.whenHealthy: RPM matches expected value for gear and speed
correlations.whenAbnormal: RPM higher than expected (slipping) or wrong gear

correlations.name: TPS1 vs TPS2 Cross-Check
correlations.description: Dual TPS sensors should track each other. Disagreement is a safety issue on drive-by-wire vehicles.
correlations.formula: TPS1 ≈ TPS2 within 2%
correlations.whenHealthy: Both TPS sensors reading identically
correlations.whenAbnormal: TPS sensors disagree — potential safety issue

correlations.name: MAP vs Barometric Pressure
correlations.description: MAP should never exceed barometric pressure on naturally aspirated engines.
correlations.formula: MAP ≤ Barometric Pressure (NA engines)
correlations.whenHealthy: MAP always below or equal to barometric
correlations.whenAbnormal: MAP exceeds barometric (sensor error or boost on NA)

correlations.name: O2 Sensor Activity vs Time
correlations.description: O2 sensors should start switching within 30 seconds of start. Delayed activity = heater failure.
correlations.formula: O2 should be switching before ECT reaches 60°C
correlations.whenHealthy: O2 sensor active and switching early in warm-up
correlations.whenAbnormal: O2 sensor slow to respond or not switching

correlations.name: DPF Differential Pressure vs Engine Load
correlations.description: DPF backpressure increases with exhaust flow (load). High DP = soot accumulation needing regen.
correlations.formula: DPF DP increases with exhaust volume
correlations.whenHealthy: Proportional increase with load, within limits
correlations.whenAbnormal: Excessive pressure drop indicating high soot load

correlations.name: DPF Temp During Regeneration
correlations.description: DPF temperature should rise significantly during active regen (550-650°C).
correlations.formula: DPF temp reaches 550-650°C during active regen
correlations.whenHealthy: Controlled temperature rise during regen
correlations.whenAbnormal: Temp not reaching regen threshold or runaway

correlations.name: System Voltage vs Electrical Load
correlations.description: Voltage should remain stable (13.5-14.8V) despite electrical load changes.
correlations.formula: Voltage should stay 13.5-14.8V regardless of load
correlations.whenHealthy: Stable voltage across all conditions
correlations.whenAbnormal: Voltage dropping under load or not recovering

correlations.name: Oil Pressure vs Engine RPM
correlations.description: Oil pressure should increase with RPM due to pump output. Low pressure at high RPM = pump wear or bearing failure.
correlations.formula: Oil pressure roughly doubles from idle to 3000 RPM
correlations.whenHealthy: Oil pressure rises smoothly with RPM
correlations.whenAbnormal: Oil pressure not increasing with RPM

correlations.name: Oil Pressure vs Oil Temperature
correlations.description: Oil pressure decreases as oil warms due to viscosity change. Excessive drop when hot = bearing wear.
correlations.formula: Oil pressure drops 20-40 kPa as oil goes from cold to hot
correlations.whenHealthy: Moderate pressure drop as oil warms
correlations.whenAbnormal: Excessive pressure drop when hot

correlations.name: Volumetric Efficiency Validation
correlations.description: Cross-check MAF, RPM, and displacement for VE calculation. Allows predicting ideal MAF vs actual.
correlations.formula: VE = (MAF × 3456) / (RPM × Displacement × Air Density)
correlations.whenHealthy: Calculated VE is 85-100% at WOT
correlations.whenAbnormal: VE significantly different from expected

correlations.name: Injector Duty Cycle vs Load
correlations.description: Injector duty cycle should match engine load demand. IDC maxing out = fuel system limitation.
correlations.formula: IDC = (Pulse Width × RPM) / 1200. Should be <85% at WOT.
correlations.whenHealthy: IDC below 85% at WOT (headroom for enrichment)
correlations.whenAbnormal: IDC maxing out, indicates fuel system limitation

correlations.name: Timing Advance vs Engine Load
correlations.description: Timing advance should decrease (retard) as load increases to prevent detonation. Monitors knock management.
correlations.formula: Timing retards ~1° per 5% load increase above 50% load
correlations.whenHealthy: Timing gradually retards under increasing load
correlations.whenAbnormal: Timing doesn't retard (knock risk) or retards excessively (power loss)
possibleCauses: Knock sensor failure causing no retard
Excessive knock retard from low octane fuel
Cooling system issue raising combustion temps

correlations.name: MAP at Idle vs Barometric
correlations.description: Idle MAP should be 25-45 kPa (strong vacuum). Ratio to barometric indicates intake sealing quality.
correlations.formula: Idle MAP ≈ 30-45% of barometric pressure for healthy NA engine
correlations.whenHealthy: Strong idle vacuum (MAP/Baro ratio 0.25-0.45)
correlations.whenAbnormal: Weak vacuum (ratio > 0.55) indicates leak or restriction
possibleCauses: Throttle body carbon buildup preventing full closure

correlations.name: IAT vs Coolant Temperature Convergence
correlations.description: During warmup, IAT should rise toward ECT as engine bay heats. No convergence = IAT sensor fault.
correlations.formula: IAT approaches ECT as under-hood temps stabilize (typically within 20°C at cruise)
correlations.whenHealthy: IAT rises with underhood temperature, stays below ECT
correlations.whenAbnormal: IAT stays cold (sensor fault) or exceeds ECT (heat soak or sensor error)
possibleCauses: Heat soak from exhaust manifold routing

correlations.name: Bank 1 vs Bank 2 Fuel Trim Symmetry
correlations.description: On V/flat engines, B1 and B2 LTFT should be within 5% of each other. Asymmetry = bank-specific issue.
correlations.formula: |LTFT_B1 - LTFT_B2| < 5% for balanced fueling
correlations.whenHealthy: Both banks compensating equally
correlations.whenAbnormal: One bank compensating significantly more than other
possibleCauses: Intake manifold gasket leak on one bank
Exhaust leak before O2 on one bank
VVT phaser issue on one bank

correlations.name: Coolant Temp vs Voltage During Warmup
correlations.description: As coolant rises during warmup, alternator field duty increases to compensate electrical load. Voltage should stabilize around 14V once warm.
correlations.formula: V_charge stabilizes as ECT approaches operating temp
correlations.whenHealthy: Voltage settles to 13.8-14.4V once ECT > 80°C
correlations.whenAbnormal: Voltage erratic or low even after warmup complete

correlations.name: RPM vs MAF at Idle Validation
correlations.description: At idle, MAF should be proportional to displacement and idle RPM. Values outside range indicate sensor drift.
correlations.formula: MAF_idle ≈ (RPM × Disp × 1.184) / 3456 for NA engines
correlations.whenHealthy: MAF 2-7 g/s at idle for typical 1.3-2.0L engines
correlations.whenAbnormal: MAF too high (leak after sensor) or too low (sensor contaminated)

correlations.name: Vehicle Speed vs Load at Cruise
correlations.description: At constant highway speed, load should be stable 30-50%. Fluctuating load at constant speed = transmission or engine issue.
correlations.formula: Load_cruise ≈ 30-50% for level highway driving
correlations.whenHealthy: Load stable at cruise, proportional to gradient/headwind
correlations.whenAbnormal: Load unusually high or fluctuating at constant speed

correlations.name: IAT vs MAF Density Correction
correlations.description: Higher intake air temp means lower air density. MAF should decrease for same throttle position as IAT rises.
correlations.formula: ρ_air ∝ 1/T → MAF decreases as IAT increases at constant throttle
correlations.whenHealthy: MAF slightly lower on hot days for same driving conditions
correlations.whenAbnormal: MAF unaffected by IAT changes — sensor not compensating

correlations.name: Throttle Position vs RPM Response
correlations.description: Opening throttle should cause proportional RPM increase. Sluggish response = mechanical or fueling issue.
correlations.formula: RPM ≈ f(TPS) with lag < 500ms
correlations.whenHealthy: RPM increases smoothly with throttle opening
correlations.whenAbnormal: RPM hesitates, surges, or doesn't respond to throttle

correlations.name: Downstream O2 vs Engine Load
correlations.description: Post-cat O2 should remain stable (0.5-0.7V) regardless of load if catalyst is functioning. Tracking load = failing catalyst.
correlations.formula: O2_post-cat should be independent of load when cat healthy
correlations.whenHealthy: Post-cat O2 stable at 0.5-0.7V across load range
correlations.whenAbnormal: Post-cat O2 follows pre-cat O2 pattern (mimicking upstream)

correlations.name: STFT vs O2 Upstream Feedback
correlations.description: STFT should be inversely related to O2 voltage — rich O2 → negative STFT correction, lean O2 → positive STFT.
correlations.formula: STFT = -K × (O2 - 0.45V) via PID controller
correlations.whenHealthy: STFT oscillates inversely with O2 sensor voltage
correlations.whenAbnormal: STFT and O2 moving in same direction or STFT pegged

correlations.name: RPM vs Voltage Under High Load
correlations.description: At high RPM/load, alternator should maintain voltage despite increased electrical demand from fans, ignition, etc.
correlations.formula: V_alt = f(RPM × pulley_ratio) - load_current
correlations.whenHealthy: Voltage stable 13.8-14.4V even under high load
correlations.whenAbnormal: Voltage drops below 13V under load despite adequate RPM

correlations.name: MAP vs RPM Pumping Relationship
correlations.description: Higher RPM at constant throttle should decrease manifold pressure (more pumping). Validates intake system integrity.
correlations.formula: MAP decreases as RPM increases at constant throttle (more vacuum)
correlations.whenHealthy: MAP drops as RPM rises at part throttle
correlations.whenAbnormal: MAP doesn't drop with RPM — vacuum leak or intake obstruction

correlations.name: Coolant Temp vs Engine Load Thermal Response
correlations.description: Sustained high load should cause gradual coolant temp rise if cooling system is at capacity.
correlations.formula: Q_engine ∝ Load → ECT rises if Q_engine > Q_cooling
correlations.whenHealthy: ECT stable 85-95°C even under moderate load
correlations.whenAbnormal: ECT rises rapidly under load — cooling capacity exceeded

correlations.name: Speed vs RPM for Gear Detection
correlations.description: Speed/RPM ratio identifies current gear. Sudden ratio changes without speed change indicate transmission issue.
correlations.formula: Gear_n = Speed / (RPM × tire_circumference × final_drive × gear_ratio_n)
correlations.whenHealthy: Discrete speed/RPM ratio steps corresponding to gear positions
correlations.whenAbnormal: Ratio slipping between gears or not matching any expected gear

correlations.name: EGR Command vs Timing Retard
correlations.description: EGR flow should correlate with less aggressive timing advance as inert gas slows combustion.

validConditions.description: Under load where EGR is active

correlations.formula: Higher EGR → slower burn rate → ECU retards timing
correlations.whenHealthy: Timing retards slightly as EGR increases
correlations.whenAbnormal: Timing unaffected by EGR changes — ECU or EGR valve fault

correlations.name: EVAP Purge vs STFT Response
correlations.description: When EVAP purge opens, fuel vapors enter intake causing momentary rich condition that STFT corrects.
correlations.formula: Purge flow → fuel vapors → rich shift → negative STFT correction
correlations.whenHealthy: STFT goes slightly negative when purge opens
correlations.whenAbnormal: No STFT response to purge — possible vapor leak or purge valve stuck

correlations.name: Oil Pressure vs Coolant Temp at Idle
correlations.description: Oil pressure drops as oil warms (viscosity decreases). Normal for hot idle to have lower pressure than cold idle.
correlations.formula: Oil_pressure ∝ viscosity(T) × RPM / clearance
correlations.whenHealthy: Oil pressure 200-400 kPa cold idle, 100-250 kPa hot idle
correlations.whenAbnormal: Oil pressure drops below 100 kPa at hot idle

correlations.name: Catalyst Temp vs Fuel Trim Interaction
correlations.description: Running rich (negative fuel trim) increases catalyst temp due to exothermic oxidation of excess fuel in catalyst.
correlations.formula: Rich mixture → unburned HC in exhaust → exothermic reaction in cat → higher cat temp
correlations.whenHealthy: Cat temp 350-600°C, slightly higher with rich conditions
correlations.whenAbnormal: Cat temp excessive (>800°C) with very rich conditions — catalyst destruction risk
possibleCauses: Misfire sending raw fuel to catalyst

correlations.name: Timing vs RPM Under Knock Conditions
correlations.description: ECU retards timing at high RPM/load if knock is detected. Healthy engine maintains advance at high RPM.
correlations.formula: Advance should increase with RPM unless knock detected
correlations.whenHealthy: Timing advance increases from 10° idle to 25-35° at 3000+ RPM
correlations.whenAbnormal: Timing retards at high RPM instead of advancing

correlations.name: Fuel Level vs LTFT Lean Trend
correlations.description: Very low fuel level can cause fuel pump to suck air, causing intermittent lean condition.
correlations.formula: Very low fuel → air ingestion → lean spikes
correlations.whenHealthy: LTFT unaffected by fuel level above 15%
correlations.whenAbnormal: LTFT goes positive (lean) when fuel level drops very low

correlations.name: IAT vs Timing Advance Correction
correlations.description: Higher intake air temps increase knock tendency. ECU should retard timing as IAT rises above normal.
correlations.formula: Higher IAT → denser charge → more knock tendency → retard timing
correlations.whenHealthy: Timing retards 1-3° for every 20°C rise in IAT above 40°C
correlations.whenAbnormal: Timing unaffected by IAT — ECU not compensating for knock risk

correlations.name: Engine Load vs Fuel Pressure
correlations.description: Direct injection systems increase fuel pressure with load. Port injection maintains constant rail pressure.
correlations.formula: Fuel pressure = base + load_modifier (DI systems)
correlations.whenHealthy: Fuel pressure rises proportionally with load demand
correlations.whenAbnormal: Fuel pressure drops under load — pump or regulator issue

correlations.name: STFT Idle vs STFT Cruise Comparison
correlations.description: STFT behavior at idle vs cruise isolates vacuum leaks (idle-only lean) from MAF errors (both conditions lean).
correlations.formula: If lean only at idle → vacuum leak; if lean everywhere → MAF/injector issue
correlations.whenHealthy: STFT similar magnitude at idle and cruise (±3%)
correlations.whenAbnormal: STFT positive at idle but normal at cruise

correlations.name: Barometric Pressure vs MAP Altitude Validation
correlations.description: Barometric and MAP at key-on/engine-off should match (both reading atmospheric). Validates both sensors.
correlations.formula: BARO ≈ MAP when engine not running (both = atmospheric)
correlations.whenHealthy: BARO and MAP within 3 kPa at engine-off
correlations.whenAbnormal: Significant difference between BARO and MAP at key-on

correlations.name: Trans Temp vs Coolant Temp Tracking
correlations.description: Trans fluid temp often cooled by shared radiator. Both should reach similar operating temps. Trans much hotter = cooler bypass.
correlations.formula: Trans_temp ≈ ECT ± 20°C when sharing cooling circuit
correlations.whenHealthy: Trans temp within 20°C of coolant temp at steady state
correlations.whenAbnormal: Trans temp much higher than coolant — trans cooler blocked or bypassed

correlations.name: Misfire Count vs RPM Range
correlations.description: Misfires concentrated at certain RPM ranges indicate specific causes — low RPM: fuel/ignition, high RPM: valve train/springs.
correlations.formula: Misfire_pattern vs RPM discriminates mechanical from electrical causes
correlations.whenHealthy: Zero misfires across all RPM ranges
correlations.whenAbnormal: Misfires at specific RPM band

correlations.name: Coolant Temp vs Vehicle Speed Cooling Effect
correlations.description: Vehicle speed provides ram air cooling to radiator. Overheating at idle but not at speed = fan problem.
correlations.formula: Increasing speed → more ram air → better cooling → lower ECT
correlations.whenHealthy: ECT drops when vehicle picks up speed from idle
correlations.whenAbnormal: ECT doesn't decrease with speed — radiator airflow blocked

correlations.name: LTFT vs Barometric Pressure Altitude Correction
correlations.description: At high altitude (low baro), engine may run slightly lean. ECU uses baro to correct, but LTFT may still shift positive.
correlations.formula: Lower baro → less O2 → lean tendency → positive LTFT drift
correlations.whenHealthy: LTFT within ±5% regardless of altitude (ECU compensating)
correlations.whenAbnormal: LTFT increasingly positive at higher altitude

correlations.name: Timing Advance vs Coolant Temp During Warmup
correlations.description: ECU advances timing as engine warms to improve efficiency. Cold engines run retarded for emissions and warmup speed.
correlations.formula: Timing_advance increases as ECT rises toward operating temp
correlations.whenHealthy: Timing advances from 5-10° cold to 15-25° warm
correlations.whenAbnormal: Timing stays retarded even as engine warms
possibleCauses: ECT sensor reading lower than actual (stuck cold)

correlations.name: MAF vs MAP Cross-Validation
correlations.description: Speed-density (MAP) and mass airflow (MAF) should agree. Disagreement isolates which sensor is faulty.
correlations.formula: Higher MAF → more air flow → higher MAP (less vacuum) at part throttle
correlations.whenHealthy: MAF and MAP move proportionally in opposite vacuum direction
correlations.whenAbnormal: MAF says high flow but MAP shows high vacuum (or vice versa)

correlations.name: Load vs RPM Efficiency Envelope
correlations.description: Engine load at given RPM should fall within expected efficiency envelope. High load at low RPM = possible engine strain.
correlations.formula: Load should scale with RPM within boundaries of VE curve
correlations.whenHealthy: Load increases proportionally with RPM under consistent throttle
correlations.whenAbnormal: Load plateaus or drops at certain RPM — breathing restriction

correlations.name: O2 B1S1 vs O2 B2S1 Tracking
correlations.description: On V-engines, both bank upstream O2 sensors should oscillate similarly. Different patterns = bank-specific issue.
correlations.formula: B1S1 and B2S1 should oscillate at similar frequency and amplitude
correlations.whenHealthy: Both bank O2 sensors switching at similar rate and amplitude
correlations.whenAbnormal: One bank stuck or lazy while other oscillates normally
possibleCauses: O2 sensor failure on one bank

correlations.name: Trans Temp vs Sustained Speed/Load
correlations.description: Transmission temp rises during sustained high-load driving (towing, hills). Should not exceed 120°C for longevity.
correlations.formula: Trans_temp ∝ ∫(torque × slip) dt
correlations.whenHealthy: Trans temp below 100°C during normal driving
correlations.whenAbnormal: Trans temp exceeds 120°C — fluid life halves every 20°F above normal

correlations.name: RPM Stability vs ECT at Idle
correlations.description: Idle RPM should be more stable when engine is warm. Cold idle may have slight hunting as ECU adjusts fuel/air.
correlations.formula: RPM_std_dev should decrease as ECT approaches operating temp
correlations.whenHealthy: Idle RPM ±20 RPM when fully warm
correlations.whenAbnormal: Idle unstable (±50+ RPM) even when warm

correlations.name: Oil Temp vs Coolant Temp Tracking
correlations.description: Oil temp should track coolant temp within ~20°C. Oil much hotter = oil cooler issue or excessive friction.
correlations.formula: Oil_temp ≈ ECT + 10-20°C (oil absorbs extra friction heat)
correlations.whenHealthy: Oil temp 10-20°C above coolant temp at steady state
correlations.whenAbnormal: Oil temp 40+°C above coolant — excessive friction or oil cooler failure

correlations.name: Voltage Drop vs Electrical Load at Idle
correlations.description: At idle, alternator output is low. Heavy electrical loads (AC, lights, fan) should not drop voltage below 12.5V.

validConditions.description: Idle where alternator output is minimal

correlations.formula: V_idle = V_alternator(RPM_idle × pulley_ratio) - I_load × R_internal
correlations.whenHealthy: Voltage stays above 13.2V at idle with normal loads
correlations.whenAbnormal: Voltage drops below 12.5V at idle

correlations.name: MAF vs Throttle During Acceleration
correlations.description: During snap throttle, MAF should spike before settling. Valve timing and turbo lag affect response shape.
correlations.formula: MAF peaks ~200ms after TPS during tip-in (intake manifold filling)
correlations.whenHealthy: MAF rises with throttle within 200ms, proportional to opening
correlations.whenAbnormal: MAF sluggish or doesn't match throttle opening magnitude

correlations.name: EGR vs Load Operating Schedule
correlations.description: EGR should be zero at idle and WOT, active 20-70% at part load. Wrong schedule = ECU or valve fault.
correlations.formula: EGR = 0 at idle/WOT, peaks at 15-30% during cruise (part load)
correlations.whenHealthy: EGR follows bell curve: 0% at idle/WOT, peak at moderate load
correlations.whenAbnormal: EGR active at idle (rough idle) or zero at cruise (high NOx)

correlations.name: Vehicle Speed vs Implied Fuel Consumption
correlations.description: At highway speeds, MAF/speed ratio indicates fuel economy. Rising ratio = increasing drag or efficiency loss.
correlations.formula: Fuel consumption ∝ MAF/14.7 (stoichiometric), normalized by speed
correlations.whenHealthy: MAF/speed ratio consistent at constant speed
correlations.whenAbnormal: MAF/speed ratio increasing over time — drag or efficiency loss

correlations.name: Catalyst Efficiency vs Drive Time
correlations.description: Catalyst must reach light-off temp (~300°C) before efficient. Short trips prevent full catalyst warmup.
correlations.formula: Cat_temp rises from ambient to 300-600°C over first 2-5 minutes
correlations.whenHealthy: Cat reaches 300°C within 2-3 minutes of cold start
correlations.whenAbnormal: Cat temp rise very slow — engine running too lean during warmup

correlations.name: Oil Pressure vs RPM During Acceleration
correlations.description: Oil pressure should rise linearly with RPM. A plateau or drop indicates pump or relief valve issue.
correlations.formula: Oil_pressure = pump_flow(RPM) × system_resistance - relief_valve_bypass
correlations.whenHealthy: Oil pressure rises smoothly to 300-500 kPa at 3000 RPM
correlations.whenAbnormal: Oil pressure plateaus or drops at higher RPM

correlations.name: Runtime vs Fuel Trim Stabilization
correlations.description: Fuel trims should stabilize within 2-5 minutes of startup. Persistent oscillation after warmup = control issue.
correlations.formula: STFT variance should decrease as engine reaches steady state
correlations.whenHealthy: STFT ±5% with low variance after warmup
correlations.whenAbnormal: STFT still swinging ±15%+ after 5 minutes

correlations.name: EGR Error vs Engine Load
correlations.description: EGR position error should be near zero regardless of load. Error increasing with load = valve sticking or feedback fault.
correlations.formula: EGR_error should remain near zero regardless of command position
correlations.whenHealthy: EGR tracks commanded position within ±3% at all loads
correlations.whenAbnormal: EGR error grows as load/command increases

correlations.name: Throttle vs MAP Inverse Relationship
correlations.description: Opening throttle reduces vacuum (raises MAP toward atmospheric). Zero throttle = maximum vacuum.
correlations.formula: MAP ≈ BARO - vacuum, vacuum ∝ 1/throttle_opening
correlations.whenHealthy: MAP rises smoothly from 30kPa (closed) to 95kPa (WOT)
correlations.whenAbnormal: MAP doesn't follow throttle — vacuum leak or MAP sensor fault

correlations.name: Coolant Temp vs Runtime Warmup Rate
correlations.description: ECT should rise from ambient to 80°C within 5-10 minutes. Slow warmup = thermostat stuck open.
correlations.formula: ECT rise rate ≈ 0.15-0.3°C/s during normal warmup
correlations.whenHealthy: ECT reaches 80°C within 5-10 minutes depending on ambient
correlations.whenAbnormal: ECT still below 70°C after 15+ minutes

correlations.name: Fuel Pressure vs RPM Scaling
correlations.description: Fuel pressure should remain stable across RPM range. Drop at high RPM = pump capacity exceeded.
correlations.formula: Fuel pressure should stay within ±5% of target across RPM range
correlations.whenHealthy: Fuel pressure stable ±5 kPa from idle to redline
correlations.whenAbnormal: Fuel pressure drops at high RPM — pump capacity issue

correlations.name: O2 Switching Frequency vs RPM
correlations.description: O2 upstream switching rate should increase with RPM as fuel control cycles faster. Constant rate = sensor issue.
correlations.formula: O2 crossing rate increases roughly proportional to fuel injection events
correlations.whenHealthy: O2 switching rate increases with RPM
correlations.whenAbnormal: O2 switching rate constant regardless of RPM — sensor aging

correlations.name: MAP vs Baro at Wide Open Throttle
correlations.description: At WOT on NA engine, MAP should approach baro (minimal restriction). Difference = intake restriction.
correlations.formula: MAP_WOT ≈ BARO - intake_restriction_pressure_drop
correlations.whenHealthy: MAP within 5 kPa of baro at WOT (NA engines)
correlations.whenAbnormal: MAP significantly below baro at WOT — air restriction

correlations.name: STFT vs LTFT Direction Agreement
correlations.description: If LTFT is positive (lean correction), STFT should oscillate around zero. If STFT is also persistently positive, correction is insufficient.
correlations.formula: STFT should center near zero when LTFT has compensated the error
correlations.whenHealthy: LTFT compensating, STFT oscillates ±3% around zero
correlations.whenAbnormal: Both STFT and LTFT large and same direction — correction limit approaching

correlations.name: Timing Advance at High Load
correlations.description: Timing should retard at high load to prevent knock. If timing stays advanced at WOT, knock sensor may be faulty.
correlations.formula: Timing retards 3-8° from MBT at high load to prevent knock
correlations.whenHealthy: Timing retards from 30° cruise to 15-20° at WOT
correlations.whenAbnormal: Timing remains advanced at high load — knock risk
possibleCauses: Wiring harness issue to knock sensor

correlations.name: Coolant-Oil Temperature Delta While Driving
correlations.description: Oil temp typically runs 10-20°C hotter than coolant. A shrinking delta may indicate thermostat issue.
correlations.formula: Oil_temp - ECT ≈ 10-20°C steady state
correlations.whenHealthy: Delta 10-20°C, stable during normal driving
correlations.whenAbnormal: Delta > 30°C or < 5°C indicates cooling circuit imbalance

correlations.name: Load vs MAF Volumetric Efficiency Check
correlations.description: Calculated load from MAF should match reported load. Disagreement reveals sensor offset.
correlations.formula: Calc_load = MAF / (MAF_max for displacement) × 100%
correlations.whenHealthy: Reported load tracks calculated load from MAF within 5%
correlations.whenAbnormal: Load and MAF-derived load diverge significantly

correlations.name: EVAP Purge Flow vs Idle Stability
correlations.description: High EVAP purge flow at idle can cause lean dip or surging. ECU should compensate with fuel trim.
correlations.formula: Purge flow adds unmetered fuel vapor → RPM dip then recovery
correlations.whenHealthy: RPM briefly dips then recovers when purge opens at idle
correlations.whenAbnormal: RPM drops significantly or stalls when purge activates
possibleCauses: EVAP purge valve flowing too much

correlations.name: RPM vs Fuel System Status During Decel
correlations.description: During deceleration (closed throttle, high RPM), ECU should cut fuel. Fuel trims should go to zero/inactive.
correlations.formula: Fuel cut activates when TPS=0 and RPM > cutoff (typically 1200-1500)
correlations.whenHealthy: Fuel system goes to decel fuel cut, trims inactive
correlations.whenAbnormal: Fuel system stays in closed loop during decel — fuel cut disabled
possibleCauses: TPS not reading zero at closed throttle
Coolant temp too low for fuel cut

correlations.name: Oil Pressure Cold vs Hot Comparison
correlations.description: Oil pressure should be higher when cold (thick oil) vs hot (thin oil). Very small delta = worn pump or bearings.
correlations.formula: P_oil ∝ μ(T) × RPM, where μ(T) decreases with temperature
correlations.whenHealthy: Cold idle pressure 300-500 kPa, hot idle pressure 150-300 kPa
correlations.whenAbnormal: Minimal pressure difference cold vs hot — worn pump or excessive clearances
possibleCauses: Oil pump worn (low output regardless of viscosity)

correlations.name: Trans Temp vs Shift Quality (RPM Flare)
correlations.description: Hot transmission fluid degrades clutch friction material performance, causing RPM flare during shifts.
correlations.formula: RPM_flare during shift increases with trans temp if clutches degraded
correlations.whenHealthy: Clean shifts with < 200 RPM flare regardless of temp
correlations.whenAbnormal: RPM flare during shifts increases when trans is hot

correlations.name: Barometric Pressure vs Load Ceiling
correlations.description: At high altitude, maximum achievable load decreases because air density is lower. Load ceiling should track baro.

validConditions.description: Near WOT to measure max load

correlations.formula: Max_load ∝ baro/101.3 (fraction of sea-level density)
correlations.whenHealthy: Peak load decreases ~3% per 300m altitude gain
correlations.whenAbnormal: Peak load much lower than expected for altitude
possibleCauses: Air intake restriction compounding altitude effect
Turbo wastegate stuck open at altitude

correlations.name: IAT + Baro vs MAF Density Validation
correlations.description: From IAT and baro, we can compute theoretical air density and compare with MAF-derived density to validate sensors.
correlations.formula: ρ_air = P_baro / (R × (IAT+273.15)); MAF = ρ_air × flow_velocity × duct_area
correlations.whenHealthy: Calculated density matches MAF-derived density within 10%
correlations.whenAbnormal: Significant disagreement between density calculations
possibleCauses: IAT sensor reading wrong location (heat soak)

correlations.name: Speed vs Timing During Deceleration
correlations.description: During engine braking (decel), timing should retard to help engine braking effect and reduce emissions.
correlations.formula: Timing retards during decel for engine braking and emission control
correlations.whenHealthy: Timing retards to near 0° during closed-throttle deceleration
correlations.whenAbnormal: Timing stays advanced during decel — poor engine braking, popping exhaust

correlations.name: Distance Since Reset vs Oil Life Indicators
correlations.description: Oil life decreases with distance, heat stress, and high RPM operation. Tracks when oil service is needed.
correlations.formula: Oil_life = 100% - f(distance, heat_stress, rpm_cycles)
correlations.whenHealthy: Oil pressure maintained regardless of distance (oil still in specification)
correlations.whenAbnormal: Oil pressure gradually declining with distance — oil degradation

correlations.name: Coolant vs IAT Heat Soak Detection
correlations.description: After engine shutdown and restart, IAT may be elevated due to heat soak from engine bay. Should normalize quickly.
correlations.formula: IAT_restart ≈ ambient + heat_soak_factor × (ECT - ambient)
correlations.whenHealthy: IAT elevated at restart but drops to near ambient within 2-3 minutes of driving
correlations.whenAbnormal: IAT stays elevated — intake routing through hot zone

correlations.name: MIL Distance vs System Severity
correlations.description: Long distance driven with MIL active suggests the condition hasn't caused immediate drivability issue but may worsen.

validConditions.description: MIL has been on for significant distance

correlations.formula: MIL distance correlates with chronic vs acute fault classification
correlations.whenHealthy: No MIL active (distance = 0 or N/A)
correlations.whenAbnormal: MIL on for 500+ km — chronic issue being ignored
possibleCauses: Intermittent fault with MIL stuck on

correlations.name: O2 Sensor Warmup vs Closed Loop Transition
correlations.description: O2 sensor must reach ~350°C before accurate readings. Closed loop should engage within 30-60s of cold start.
correlations.formula: O2 sensor should begin oscillating within 30-60s after start
correlations.whenHealthy: O2 sensor active and switching within 30-60 seconds
correlations.whenAbnormal: O2 still stuck at 0.45V after 90 seconds — heater or sensor fault

correlations.name: Load vs Speed for Grade Detection
correlations.description: At constant speed, load increasing indicates uphill grade. Used to distinguish driving conditions from engine issues.
correlations.formula: Load_grade = Load_flat + m × g × sin(θ) / (engine_power/speed)
correlations.whenHealthy: Load varies with road grade, stable on flat road
correlations.whenAbnormal: Load increasing at constant speed on flat road

correlations.name: MAF vs Fuel Trim Stoichiometric Balance
correlations.description: If MAF is accurate and injectors are correct, fuel trims should be near zero. Together they validate the air-fuel path.
correlations.formula: Injector_PW × fuel_pressure / MAF should yield 14.7:1 AFR when trims are 0
correlations.whenHealthy: LTFT near 0% indicates MAF and injectors agree on air mass
correlations.whenAbnormal: Large LTFT = MAF or injectors have systematic error

correlations.name: Trans Temp Dissipation at Idle
correlations.description: When stopped after highway driving, trans temp should slowly decrease if cooler is working. Rising temp at idle = problem.
correlations.formula: Trans_temp should decrease at rate ≈ 0.5-1°C/min at idle with cooler working
correlations.whenHealthy: Trans temp decreases slowly at idle
correlations.whenAbnormal: Trans temp rises at idle — torque converter generating heat without forward motion

correlations.name: RPM vs Load During Acceleration
correlations.description: During acceleration, RPM and load should rise together. Load rising without RPM = slipping; RPM rising without load = no combustion torque.
correlations.formula: RPM and load should co-vary during tip-in acceleration
correlations.whenHealthy: Both RPM and load rise proportionally during acceleration
correlations.whenAbnormal: RPM flares without load increase, or load rises with no RPM response
possibleCauses: Transmission clutch slip (RPM rises, load doesn't)

correlations.name: Throttle Position vs Timing Advance Coordination
correlations.description: ECU coordinates timing with throttle — WOT should have less advance (knock prevention). Light throttle allows more advance.
correlations.formula: Timing retards as throttle increases to prevent knock at higher cylinder pressures
correlations.whenHealthy: Timing advance decreases as throttle opens wider
correlations.whenAbnormal: Timing remains advanced at high throttle — knock risk

correlations.name: Coolant Temp vs Timing During Cold Start
correlations.description: Cold engines use retarded timing to increase exhaust heat for faster catalyst light-off and reduce knock risk with cold chamber walls.
correlations.formula: Timing advances from retarded cold value toward normal as ECT increases
correlations.whenHealthy: Timing progresses from 5-10° cold to 15-25° as engine warms
correlations.whenAbnormal: Timing stuck retarded despite engine reaching operating temp

correlations.name: MAF vs RPM for Volumetric Efficiency
correlations.description: VE = (MAF_actual / MAF_theoretical) × 100. Typical NA engine peaks at 80-90% VE. Low VE = breathing restriction.
correlations.formula: VE = MAF / (RPM × Disp × ρ_air / 3456) × 100
correlations.whenHealthy: VE 80-90% for NA engines at WOT across RPM range
correlations.whenAbnormal: VE significantly below 70% at WOT — restrictive condition

correlations.name: Speed vs MAF Aerodynamic Drag Effect
correlations.description: At constant crusing, MAF increases with speed squared (aerodynamic drag). Validates engine is correctly compensating for road load.

validConditions.description: Highway speed where aero drag dominates

correlations.formula: Road load = C_rr × m × g + 0.5 × ρ × Cd × A × v², fuel ∝ road_load
correlations.whenHealthy: MAF increases progressively with speed at cruise
correlations.whenAbnormal: MAF disproportionately high for speed — excessive drag

correlations.name: STFT vs MAP for Vacuum Leak Detection
correlations.description: Vacuum leak causes both higher MAP (less vacuum) and positive STFT at idle. This dual-parameter check isolates vacuum leaks from other lean causes.

validConditions.description: At idle where vacuum leaks have most effect

correlations.formula: Vacuum leak → higher MAP + lean → positive STFT; both move together
correlations.whenHealthy: Low MAP (high vacuum) at idle with STFT near zero
correlations.whenAbnormal: Both MAP elevated and STFT positive at idle

correlations.name: Voltage vs RPM for Belt Condition
correlations.description: Alternator is belt-driven — voltage should track RPM. If voltage doesn't rise with RPM, belt is slipping or alternator failing.
correlations.formula: V_alt ∝ alternator_RPM = engine_RPM × pulley_ratio
correlations.whenHealthy: Voltage increases from 13.5V at idle to 14.2V at 2500 RPM
correlations.whenAbnormal: Voltage flat or decreasing as RPM rises

correlations.name: Coolant Temp vs Baro for Boiling Point Margin
correlations.description: At altitude, coolant boiling point drops ~3°C per 1000m. Overheating thresholds must account for barometric pressure.
correlations.formula: Boiling point = 100°C at 101.3kPa; drops ~3°C per 1000m altitude
correlations.whenHealthy: Coolant temp stays well below altitude-adjusted boiling point
correlations.whenAbnormal: Coolant near boiling at altitude despite being below 100°C
possibleCauses: Insufficient radiator cap pressure at altitude

correlations.name: EGR vs RPM Flow Scheduling
correlations.description: EGR is disabled at idle and high RPM, active at mid-range cruise RPM. Validates EGR operating schedule correctness.
correlations.formula: EGR = 0 at idle/WOT RPM, peaks at 2000-3000 RPM cruise
correlations.whenHealthy: EGR follows expected RPM-based schedule from ECU map
correlations.whenAbnormal: EGR active at wrong RPM range or inactive when expected

correlations.name: MAP vs Load Agreement Check
correlations.description: On speed-density systems, load is calculated from MAP. They should agree closely. Disagreement = sensor calibration drift.
correlations.formula: Load_calc = (MAP / BARO) × (engine_temp_correction) for speed-density systems
correlations.whenHealthy: Load tracks MAP proportionally within ±5%
correlations.whenAbnormal: MAP and load diverge — sensor or ECU calculation error

correlations.name: Fuel Pressure vs Load Demand Profile
correlations.description: High load demands more fuel flow. Fuel pressure must be maintained despite higher injector duty. Drop under load = pump capacity issue.
correlations.formula: Fuel pressure should remain within ±5kPa of target regardless of load
correlations.whenHealthy: Fuel pressure rock-steady as load varies
correlations.whenAbnormal: Fuel pressure drops as load increases

correlations.name: O2 Upstream vs Timing Advance Interaction
correlations.description: Leaning back from knock retard should show O2 sensor response. Validates closed-loop combustion control chain.
correlations.formula: Timing changes → combustion efficiency changes → O2 voltage shifts
correlations.whenHealthy: O2 responds to timing changes within 2-3 combustion cycles
correlations.whenAbnormal: O2 unaffected by timing changes — sensor or fueling problem
possibleCauses: Injector flow compensation masking timing effect

correlations.name: Speed vs Trans Temp on Highway Grades
correlations.description: At constant high speed, trans temp should be stable. Rising temp at constant speed = grade-induced slip or cooler issue.
correlations.formula: Stable speed should yield stable trans temp if TC locked and no grade
correlations.whenHealthy: Trans temp steady ±2°C at constant highway speed
correlations.whenAbnormal: Trans temp rising steadily at constant speed on level road

correlations.name: Runtime vs ECT Warmup Profile
correlations.description: ECT should follow a predictable warmup curve based on runtime. Deviation indicates thermostat or cooling issue.
correlations.formula: ECT ≈ IAT + (operating_temp - IAT) × (1 - e^(-t/τ)), τ ≈ 300s
correlations.whenHealthy: ECT follows exponential warmup curve reaching 80°C in 5-8 minutes
correlations.whenAbnormal: ECT stalls at partial warmup or rises too slowly

correlations.name: Baro + IAT Air Density Calculation
correlations.description: Air density ρ = P / (R × T) determines engine power ceiling. Cross-checking computed density vs actual MAF validates sensor chain.
correlations.whenHealthy: Computed air density matches MAF-derived density ±10%
correlations.whenAbnormal: Air density calculation and MAF disagree significantly
possibleCauses: IAT sensor in wrong location (heat soak)
Baro sensor stuck at sea level value

correlations.name: RPM vs Timing Advance Mapping
correlations.description: Timing advance map is primarily RPM-indexed. Higher RPM needs more advance to account for shorter combustion time.

validConditions.description: Part load for clean timing curve

correlations.formula: Base timing increases roughly linearly with RPM: ~10° at 1000 RPM to 30° at 4000 RPM
correlations.whenHealthy: Timing advance increases with RPM along expected curve
correlations.whenAbnormal: Timing advance flat or decreasing with RPM increase
possibleCauses: Knock limiting active across RPM range

correlations.name: Load vs EGR for NOx Reduction Strategy
correlations.description: EGR dilutes intake charge with inert exhaust gas to lower peak combustion temp, reducing NOx. Most effective at part load.
correlations.formula: EGR peaks at 15-25% during cruise (30-60% load), zero at idle and WOT
correlations.whenHealthy: EGR follows load-based schedule from ECU calibration
correlations.whenAbnormal: EGR active at WOT (power loss) or absent at cruise (high NOx)

correlations.name: Speed vs Load for Rolling Resistance Detection
correlations.description: At low speeds, rolling resistance dominates load. Abnormally high load at low speed = mechanical drag.

validConditions.description: Low-medium speed where rolling resistance dominates

correlations.formula: Rolling resistance force = C_rr × m × g, approximately constant with speed
correlations.whenHealthy: Load 15-30% at steady 40 km/h on flat road
correlations.whenAbnormal: Load above 40% at low constant speed on level ground

correlations.name: O2 Downstream Stability vs Catalyst Temp Light-Off
correlations.description: Catalyst must reach light-off temp (~250-300°C) before it becomes effective. Downstream O2 should stabilize after light-off.
correlations.formula: Post-cat O2 variance should decrease sharply once cat temp exceeds 300°C
correlations.whenHealthy: Downstream O2 stabilizes to 0.5-0.7V once catalyst is lit off
correlations.whenAbnormal: Downstream O2 still fluctuating after cat temp exceeds 350°C

correlations.name: Voltage vs Coolant for Fan Electrical Load Detection
correlations.description: When cooling fan activates (ECT > 95°C typically), it draws 10-30A. Voltage should dip slightly when fan engages.

validConditions.description: Near idle where fan load is most noticeable

correlations.formula: V_drop = I_fan × R_circuit, typically 0.1-0.3V drop when fan engages
correlations.whenHealthy: Small voltage dip when fan activates, alternator compensates
correlations.whenAbnormal: Large voltage drop (>0.5V) when fan activates — alternator struggling

correlations.name: MAF vs IAT for Temperature-Corrected Airflow
correlations.description: MAF measures mass flow directly but should still show IAT correlation since hot air is less dense at same throttle.
correlations.formula: MAF_corrected = MAF × (T_standard / T_actual) for density normalization
correlations.whenHealthy: MAF decreases slightly on hotter days for same driving conditions
correlations.whenAbnormal: MAF insensitive to IAT changes — possible sensor contamination
possibleCauses: MAF hot-wire element contaminated (reads low regardless)
IAT sensor in engine bay (reading engine heat, not intake)

correlations.name: Fuel Pressure vs System Voltage (Pump Health)
correlations.description: Electric fuel pump speed is voltage-dependent. Low system voltage = lower pump output = potential lean condition.
correlations.formula: Pump_flow ∝ pump_RPM ∝ voltage_applied (for brushed DC pump motors)
correlations.whenHealthy: Fuel pressure stable regardless of voltage (pump has margin)
correlations.whenAbnormal: Fuel pressure drops when voltage is low — pump running near capacity
possibleCauses: Fuel pump brushes worn (needs more voltage for same output)

correlations.name: Trans Temp vs Runtime Thermal Profile
correlations.description: Transmission fluid should reach operating temp (70-90°C) within 10-15 minutes. Trans fluids thicken dramatically when cold.
correlations.formula: Trans temp ≈ ambient + (target - ambient) × (1 - e^(-t/600))
correlations.whenHealthy: Trans fluid reaches 80°C within 15 minutes of driving
correlations.whenAbnormal: Trans fluid still below 60°C after 20 minutes

correlations.name: Oil Temp vs Load for Thermal Stress Analysis
correlations.description: High sustained load increases oil temperature. Oil above 120°C starts losing lubricity and accelerating oxidation.
correlations.formula: Oil_temp = base_temp + K × ∫(load × RPM) dt / cooling_capacity
correlations.whenHealthy: Oil stays below 120°C even under sustained moderate load
correlations.whenAbnormal: Oil temp exceeds 120°C under normal driving loads

correlations.name: MAP vs RPM at Idle for Vacuum Check
correlations.description: At idle, MAP should be low (high vacuum). MAP > 50kPa at idle indicates vacuum leak or significant engine load.
correlations.formula: Idle vacuum = BARO - MAP_idle; healthy 4-cyl: 55-75 kPa vacuum
correlations.whenHealthy: MAP 25-45 kPa at idle (55-75 kPa vacuum)
correlations.whenAbnormal: MAP > 55 kPa at idle — insufficient vacuum

correlations.name: Coolant vs Oil Warmup Rate Comparison
correlations.description: Coolant warms faster than oil due to closer proximity to combustion chamber. Oil warming faster = possible coolant flow issue.
correlations.formula: ECT should lead oil temp by 10-20°C during warmup phase
correlations.whenHealthy: Coolant leads oil temp and both rise together
correlations.whenAbnormal: Oil warming faster than coolant — coolant may not be circulating
possibleCauses: Thermostat stuck closed with bypass blocked

correlations.name: STFT vs RPM Profile Analysis
correlations.description: STFT that varies significantly with RPM indicates a speed-dependent fueling issue vs a constant offset (injector/MAF).
correlations.formula: STFT should be RPM-independent if system is calibrated correctly
correlations.whenHealthy: STFT consistent ±3% across RPM range
correlations.whenAbnormal: STFT varies systematically with RPM (e.g., lean at idle, rich at cruise)

correlations.name: Voltage vs IAT Cold Start Electrical Demand
correlations.description: Cold starts draw more electrical current (glow plugs, heated sensors, high viscosity power steering). Voltage should recover quickly.
correlations.formula: Cold start current draw can be 50-100A higher than warm; voltage recovery time indicates battery/alternator health
correlations.whenHealthy: Voltage recovers to >13.5V within 30 seconds of start
correlations.whenAbnormal: Voltage stays below 13V for extended period after cold start

correlations.name: Timing vs Catalyst Temp Over-Temperature Protection
correlations.description: If catalyst gets too hot (>900°C), ECU retards timing and enriches mixture to protect it. Should see timing drop with rising cat temp.
correlations.formula: Above 800°C cat temp, ECU applies protection retard and enrichment
correlations.whenHealthy: Timing retards as cat temp approaches limits
correlations.whenAbnormal: No timing protection despite very high cat temp

correlations.name: MAF vs RPM Idle Airflow Stability
correlations.description: At stable idle RPM, MAF should be stable. Fluctuating MAF at stable RPM indicates sensor noise or intermittent leak.
correlations.formula: MAF_idle = RPM × Disp × ρ × VE_idle / 3456, should be very stable
correlations.whenHealthy: MAF steady ±0.3 g/s at stable idle
correlations.whenAbnormal: MAF fluctuating > ±1 g/s at idle

correlations.name: Load vs Trans Temp for Towing Detection
correlations.description: Sustained high load + rising trans temp pattern indicates towing. Useful for context-aware analysis and oil life calculation.

validConditions.description: Sustained high load indicating towing or hill climbing

correlations.formula: High load × time = heat generation in TC and clutch packs
correlations.whenHealthy: Trans temp below 100°C even under sustained 70% load
correlations.whenAbnormal: Trans temp exceeds 110°C during sustained high load driving
possibleCauses: TC lockup not engaging while towing

correlations.name: Baro vs Voltage Altitude Compensation
correlations.description: At altitude, alternator can produce marginally less power (thinner air cooling, lower engine power). Validates electrical system margin.
correlations.formula: Alternator cooling efficiency decreases with air density at altitude
correlations.whenHealthy: Voltage maintained >13.5V regardless of altitude
correlations.whenAbnormal: Voltage drops at altitude, indicating alternator near thermal limit
possibleCauses: Belt slip worsens with reduced cooling

correlations.name: EGR vs Coolant for Warmup Enable Threshold
correlations.description: EGR should only activate once engine is warm (ECT > 65-80°C). EGR during warmup causes rough idle and high HC emissions.
correlations.formula: EGR enable temp typically 65-80°C depending on manufacturer
correlations.whenHealthy: Zero EGR below 65°C, gradually increasing above enable temp
correlations.whenAbnormal: EGR active during cold operation — rough idle and high HC
possibleCauses: ECT sensor reading higher than actual

correlations.name: Speed vs ECT Ram Air Cooling Effect
correlations.description: Vehicle speed provides forced airflow through radiator. ECT should decrease when transitioning from idle to highway speed.

validConditions.description: Engine warm enough for cooling to be relevant

correlations.formula: Cooling_capacity ∝ airflow ∝ max(fan_speed, ram_air_from_vehicle_speed)
correlations.whenHealthy: ECT drops 3-5°C when going from idle to 80 km/h
correlations.whenAbnormal: ECT unchanged or rises with speed — radiator airflow blocked
possibleCauses: Fan shroud directing air around radiator

correlations.name: Timing vs Fuel Trim Combined Knock Response
correlations.description: Under knock, ECU retards timing AND may add fuel (enrichment). Both parameters moving together confirms knock control active.
correlations.formula: Knock → retard timing + enrich mixture (STFT goes negative)
correlations.whenHealthy: Timing retard accompanied by slight enrichment when knock occurs
correlations.whenAbnormal: Timing retards but no corresponding fuel enrichment
possibleCauses: Fuel system already at enrichment limit

correlations.name: RPM vs Catalyst Temp Exhaust Energy
correlations.description: Higher RPM pumps more exhaust energy into catalyst, raising its temperature. Validates catalyst temp sensor responds to engine output.
correlations.formula: Cat_temp ∝ exhaust_enthalpy_flow = ṁ_exhaust × Cp × T_exhaust
correlations.whenHealthy: Cat temp rises 50-100°C when going from idle to 3000 RPM
correlations.whenAbnormal: Cat temp unresponsive to RPM changes — sensor or catalyst issue

correlations.name: IAT vs EVAP Purge Vapor Density Effect
correlations.description: Hot intake air increases fuel vapor density from EVAP purge. Higher IAT + high purge = richer vapor contribution to intake.
correlations.formula: Vapor density in canister ∝ temperature → more fuel mass per purge cycle
correlations.whenHealthy: STFT compensates for purge vapor within ±5%
correlations.whenAbnormal: Rich spike when purge activates on hot days
possibleCauses: EVAP purge too aggressive for conditions

correlations.name: Oil Temp vs RPM for Friction Heat Assessment
correlations.description: Higher RPM generates more friction heat. Oil temp should correlate with sustained RPM level, indicating lubrication system load.
correlations.formula: Friction power loss ∝ μ × N × (bearing load), generating heat absorbed by oil
correlations.whenHealthy: Oil temp rises moderately with sustained high RPM
correlations.whenAbnormal: Oil temp spikes rapidly with RPM — excessive friction

correlations.name: Voltage vs Speed for Alternator Output Profile
correlations.description: At highway speed, alternator is spinning fast and should easily maintain 14+ V. Low voltage at speed = internal alternator fault.
correlations.formula: At highway RPM, alternator should be well above knee of output curve
correlations.whenHealthy: Voltage solid 14.0-14.5V at highway speeds
correlations.whenAbnormal: Voltage below 13.5V despite highway speeds (high alternator RPM)

correlations.name: Coolant vs Catalyst Temp Exhaust Path Delta
correlations.description: Catalyst temp should always exceed coolant temp during driving. If coolant is hotter than catalyst, cat temp sensor likely faulty.
correlations.formula: Cat_temp >> ECT during operation (exhaust gas 400-800°C vs coolant 80-100°C)
correlations.whenHealthy: Cat temp 300-700°C while coolant is 80-100°C
correlations.whenAbnormal: Cat temp reported below coolant temp — sensor fault

correlations.name: MAP vs Fuel Trim Vacuum System Correlation
correlations.description: MAP and fuel trims are both affected by vacuum leaks. A vacuum leak raises MAP and drives trims lean simultaneously.

validConditions.description: Near idle where effects are greatest

correlations.formula: Vacuum leak → MAP increases AND unmetered air → positive LTFT
correlations.whenHealthy: Normal idle MAP (30-45kPa) with LTFT near 0%
correlations.whenAbnormal: Elevated idle MAP with positive LTFT — classic vacuum leak signature

correlations.name: Baro vs RPM for Power Density Assessment
correlations.description: At altitude, maximum engine power decreases proportionally with air density. High RPM may not produce expected power output.
correlations.formula: Power_actual ≈ Power_sea_level × (P_baro / 101.3) for NA engines
correlations.whenHealthy: Engine reaches expected RPM; power loss proportional to altitude
correlations.whenAbnormal: RPM limited or load excessive for altitude-adjusted expectations

correlations.name: Speed vs Fuel Pressure Return System
correlations.description: Returnless fuel systems rely on pump speed modulation. Fuel pressure should remain constant regardless of vehicle speed.
correlations.formula: Fuel pressure should be independent of vehicle speed in closed-loop fuel control
correlations.whenHealthy: Fuel pressure constant ±3kPa regardless of speed
correlations.whenAbnormal: Fuel pressure varies with vehicle speed — vibration or pump issue

correlations.name: Load vs Oil Pressure for Bearing Stress
correlations.description: High engine load increases crankshaft bearing loads, which can temporarily reduce oil film pressure. Validates oil system margin.
correlations.formula: Oil film thickness = f(viscosity, RPM, bearing_load), load increases bearing_load
correlations.whenHealthy: Oil pressure maintained under load (pump output > bearing demand)
correlations.whenAbnormal: Oil pressure drops under heavy load — pump at capacity
possibleCauses: Oil viscosity too low for load

correlations.name: Trans Load vs Shift Pattern Detection
correlations.description: Transmission shift points are load-dependent — higher load delays upshifts for more torque. Validates adaptive shift strategy.
correlations.formula: Shift_point_speed = f(throttle, load) — higher load/throttle = higher shift speed
correlations.whenHealthy: Shifts occur at expected speeds for given load level
correlations.whenAbnormal: Early or late shifts inconsistent with load level
possibleCauses: Throttle position signal to TCM error

correlations.name: ECT vs EVAP Purge Temperature Enable
correlations.description: EVAP purge only activates once engine is warm enough for stable fuel control. Purge during warmup causes overfueling.
correlations.formula: Purge enable typically >65°C ECT and in closed loop
correlations.whenHealthy: No purge before 65°C; gradually increasing purge after enable temp
correlations.whenAbnormal: Purge active during cold operation or absent when warm

correlations.name: Oil Pressure vs Voltage for Electric Oil Pump
correlations.description: Some modern engines have electrically assisted oil pumps. Low voltage can reduce pump output, especially at idle.

validConditions.description: At idle where pump output is lowest

correlations.formula: Electric pump flow ∝ motor_RPM ∝ applied_voltage
correlations.whenHealthy: Oil pressure maintained regardless of voltage fluctuations
correlations.whenAbnormal: Oil pressure drops when voltage dips — pump electrically limited

correlations.name: MAF vs Catalyst Temp Exhaust Energy Correlation
correlations.description: More airflow (MAF) means more combustion energy and higher exhaust gas temperature reaching the catalyst.
correlations.formula: Q_exhaust = ṁ_air × (1 + 1/14.7) × Cp × T_exhaust
correlations.whenHealthy: Cat temp tracks MAF proportionally under steady conditions
correlations.whenAbnormal: Cat temp doesn't respond to MAF changes — cat sensor or exhaust issue
possibleCauses: Exhaust leak between engine and cat
Cat temp sensor lag or fault

correlations.name: Load vs Fuel Economy Indication
correlations.description: Average engine load directly correlates with fuel consumption. Tracking load vs speed gives fuel economy proxy without direct fuel flow measurement.
correlations.formula: Fuel consumption ≈ MAF/14.7/density_fuel; Load/speed = inverse fuel economy proxy
correlations.whenHealthy: Load/speed ratio consistent with vehicle weight and conditions
correlations.whenAbnormal: Load/speed ratio significantly higher than expected

correlations.name: IAT + MAP Combined Charge Density
correlations.description: Intake charge density depends on both temperature and pressure. Low IAT + high MAP = dense charge (more power). Useful for turbo monitoring.
correlations.formula: ρ_charge = MAP / (R × (IAT + 273.15)); power ∝ ρ × VE × displacement
correlations.whenHealthy: Charge density appropriate for conditions
correlations.whenAbnormal: High IAT + High MAP = intercooler failure (turbo) or heat soak

correlations.name: Runtime vs Oil Pressure Long-Term Trend
correlations.description: Oil pressure should stabilize once warm and remain constant. Slowly declining oil pressure over time indicates wear.
correlations.formula: Oil pressure should be stable once oil temp stabilizes
correlations.whenHealthy: Oil pressure constant ±10kPa after warmup period
correlations.whenAbnormal: Oil pressure slowly declining over time at constant RPM
possibleCauses: Oil temperature rising beyond optimal (viscosity drop)

correlations.name: Throttle vs Speed for Cruise Control Validation
correlations.description: Under cruise control, throttle should make small adjustments to maintain constant speed. Large throttle swings = control instability.
correlations.formula: Cruise control: TPS varies minimally (±2%) for speed ±1 km/h on flat road
correlations.whenHealthy: TPS varies ±2% to maintain speed within ±1 km/h
correlations.whenAbnormal: TPS swinging >5% to maintain speed — engine response issue

correlations.name: LTFT vs Runtime for Learning Trend
correlations.description: LTFT represents long-term fuel correction. It should converge toward a stable value over driving time as ECU learns.

validConditions.description: ECU has had time to learn

correlations.formula: LTFT updates slowly based on accumulated STFT correction demand
correlations.whenHealthy: LTFT stabilizes within first 5-10 minutes of driving
correlations.whenAbnormal: LTFT still drifting after 15+ minutes — ongoing unaddressed issue

correlations.name: MAF vs Baro for Altitude Airflow Adjustment
correlations.description: At altitude, MAF reads lower for same throttle opening because air is less dense. Validates MAF is responding to actual conditions.
correlations.formula: MAF_altitude = MAF_sea_level × (P_baro / 101.3)
correlations.whenHealthy: MAF decreases proportionally with altitude (lower baro)
correlations.whenAbnormal: MAF doesn't change with altitude — sensor not measuring actual mass flow
possibleCauses: MAF element contaminated (measuring heat transfer, not mass)

correlations.name: Trans Temp vs Oil Temp Parallel Thermal Profile
correlations.description: Both fluids warm at similar rates. Trans temp much hotter than oil = trans-specific heat generation; oil much hotter = engine issue.
correlations.formula: Both fluids reach thermal equilibrium at similar temps under normal conditions
correlations.whenHealthy: Trans temp within 20°C of oil temp at steady state
correlations.whenAbnormal: Delta > 30°C between trans and oil temps
possibleCauses: Trans clutch slip generating excess heat

correlations.name: MAP vs Baro for Turbo Boost Detection
correlations.description: On turbocharged engines, MAP can exceed baro (positive boost). MAP/baro ratio indicates boost pressure in relative terms.
correlations.formula: Boost_relative = MAP - BARO; positive = boost, negative = vacuum
correlations.whenHealthy: MAP exceeds BARO under load on turbo engines (positive boost)
correlations.whenAbnormal: MAP never exceeds BARO on turbo engine — boost system failure

correlations.name: Fuel Level vs Runtime for Consumption Rate
correlations.description: Fuel level should decrease over time proportional to average load. Rapid decrease = high consumption; no decrease = sender stuck.

validConditions.description: At least 30 minutes for measurable change

correlations.formula: Consumption_rate = ΔFuel_level / Δtime × tank_capacity
correlations.whenHealthy: Fuel level decreases at rate consistent with driving conditions
correlations.whenAbnormal: Fuel level unchanging after extended driving — sender unit stuck

correlations.name: EGR vs Catalyst Temp NOx/HC Tradeoff
correlations.description: EGR reduces peak combustion temp (lower NOx) but can increase HC if excessive. Cat temp drops slightly with more EGR.
correlations.formula: More EGR → lower combustion temp → lower exhaust gas temp → lower cat temp
correlations.whenHealthy: Cat temp drops 20-40°C with EGR at 20%
correlations.whenAbnormal: Cat temp unaffected by EGR — EGR not actually flowing despite being commanded
possibleCauses: EGR valve not opening despite command

correlations.name: RPM vs Trans Temp at Extended Idle
correlations.description: Extended idle with transmission in gear creates torque converter stall heat. Trans temp should not rise excessively at idle.
correlations.formula: TC stall absorbs idle torque as heat = RPM² × K_factor × fluid_density
correlations.whenHealthy: Trans temp stable or slowly rising at extended idle (< 0.5°C/min)
correlations.whenAbnormal: Trans temp rising rapidly at idle — excessive TC slip or idle too high
possibleCauses: Torque converter stator one-way clutch failure

correlations.name: Coolant vs Fuel Trim During Warmup Enrichment
correlations.description: Cold engine needs enrichment (negative STFT). As ECT rises, enrichment tapers off and trims should approach zero at ~70°C.
correlations.formula: Enrichment multiplier decreases as ECT increases: ~1.4× at 0°C to 1.0× at 70°C
correlations.whenHealthy: STFT gradually moves from negative (rich) toward zero as engine warms
correlations.whenAbnormal: STFT still very negative at 70°C — enrichment not tapering

correlations.name: Timing vs MAP for Combustion Stability
correlations.description: Timing advance is indexed to manifold pressure (load). Lower MAP (higher vacuum) allows more advance; higher MAP requires retard.
correlations.formula: Timing_base = f(RPM, MAP); higher MAP → less advance for knock prevention
correlations.whenHealthy: Timing decreases as MAP increases (more load = less advance)
correlations.whenAbnormal: Timing doesn't retard with increasing MAP — knock risk
possibleCauses: MAP signal not reaching ECU properly

correlations.name: Load vs MAF for Intake Restriction Detection
correlations.description: If load is high but MAF is low, the engine is working hard to move a restricted air charge. Discriminates intake from exhaust restrictions.
correlations.formula: Load ∝ MAF for a given RPM; load high + MAF low = intake restriction
correlations.whenHealthy: Load and MAF move proportionally across driving conditions
correlations.whenAbnormal: High load with low MAF — breathing restriction

correlations.name: O2 Downstream vs Runtime Long-Term Drift
correlations.description: Post-cat O2 should remain stable over time. A slowly drifting post-cat O2 indicates catalyst degradation in real-time.
correlations.formula: Post-cat O2 should be stable ±0.05V over time if cat is healthy
correlations.whenHealthy: Post-cat O2 stable at 0.5-0.7V for entire drive
correlations.whenAbnormal: Post-cat O2 slowly trending lean or rich over time — cat degrading

correlations.name: Throttle vs Load Response Lag
correlations.description: Load should follow throttle position changes with minimal lag. Excessive delay indicates intake restriction or VVT delay.
correlations.formula: Load response time typically 100-200ms after throttle change in healthy engines
correlations.whenHealthy: Load follows throttle changes within 2-3 samples
correlations.whenAbnormal: Load lags throttle significantly — breathing restriction or VVT fault
possibleCauses: EGR valve stuck open at WOT

correlations.name: RPM vs Voltage During Cranking
correlations.description: Cranking voltage indicates battery and starter condition. Voltage drops below 10V with slow cranking RPM indicates battery or starter weakness.
correlations.formula: Vcranking = Vbattery - (Istarter × Rinternal); healthy >10V at >200 RPM crank
correlations.whenHealthy: Cranking voltage >10.5V with brisk starter RPM (>200 RPM)
correlations.whenAbnormal: Cranking voltage <10V with slow cranking — battery or starter degraded

correlations.name: Timing vs RPM for Idle Stability Assessment
correlations.description: At idle, timing should be stable. Timing oscillations coupled with RPM fluctuations indicate combustion instability.
correlations.formula: Timing jitter at idle should be <2° peak-to-peak with stable RPM
correlations.whenHealthy: Timing ±1° and RPM ±25 at stable idle
correlations.whenAbnormal: Timing fluctuating >3° with corresponding RPM instability

correlations.name: Coolant vs Trans Temp Shared Cooler Efficiency
correlations.description: Many vehicles share coolant and trans cooler circuits. Trans temp should track below coolant temp. If trans exceeds coolant, cooler is restricted.
correlations.formula: Trans cooled via engine coolant: trans_temp should be within 10-20°C of coolant
correlations.whenHealthy: Trans temp within 15°C of coolant temp at steady state
correlations.whenAbnormal: Trans temp exceeds coolant by >25°C — cooler restriction
possibleCauses: Coolant not flowing through trans cooler

correlations.name: MAF vs Throttle for Intake Path Validation
correlations.description: MAF should increase proportionally with throttle opening. MAF not increasing with throttle = sensor fault or severe restriction.
correlations.formula: MAF ∝ throttle_area × √(ΔP) at sub-critical flow (TPS < 80%)
correlations.whenHealthy: MAF increases smoothly with throttle opening
correlations.whenAbnormal: MAF flat despite throttle opening — sensor or restriction
possibleCauses: MAF sensor failed to fixed voltage
Severe intake restriction downstream of MAF

correlations.name: Fuel Level Signal vs Oil Pressure During Cornering
correlations.description: If both fuel level and oil pressure fluctuate simultaneously during turns, indicates fluid movement — normal for fuel but concerning for oil.
correlations.formula: Lateral G-forces cause fluid slosh; fuel sender fluctuates, oil should have baffled pickup
correlations.whenHealthy: Fuel level may fluctuate slightly; oil pressure should remain stable
correlations.whenAbnormal: Both fuel and oil pressure drop during the same events

correlations.name: EGR Error vs Runtime for Degradation Tracking
correlations.description: EGR position error should be near zero at all times. Error growing with runtime indicates valve sticking from carbon buildup.
correlations.formula: EGR_error = commanded - actual position; should stay near zero
correlations.whenHealthy: EGR error <3% throughout driving session
correlations.whenAbnormal: EGR error increases over time during session — thermal sticking
possibleCauses: Carbon buildup on EGR valve seat
EGR position sensor drift with heat
